title: Lesson Quiz: The Executive Branch — Presidential powers, enforcement of laws, and leadership roles.
description: Assessment of the concept that Presidential powers, enforcement of laws, and leadership roles.

questions.prompt: What is the main job of the executive branch?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Making laws is the role of the legislative branch.
Incorrect. Interpreting laws is the role of the judicial branch.

choices.text: To carry out and enforce laws
choices.feedback: Correct. The executive branch is responsible for enforcing laws.
Incorrect. Court decisions are part of the judicial branch.

questions.prompt: The president leads the executive branch.

choices.feedback: Correct. The president is the leader of the executive branch.
Incorrect. The president does lead the executive branch.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows the executive branch in action?

choices.text: Congress debates and votes on a bill
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is the legislative branch.

choices.text: A court reviews a law for constitutionality
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is the judicial branch.

choices.text: The president enforces a law across the country
choices.feedback: Correct. Enforcing laws is a key role of the executive branch.

choices.text: Judges interpret the meaning of a law
choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is the judicial branch.

questions.prompt: The executive branch is responsible for making laws.

choices.feedback: Incorrect. The executive branch enforces laws, not makes them.
Correct. Making laws is the role of the legislative branch.

questions.prompt: Why is the president’s leadership role important in the executive branch?

choices.text: Because the president writes all laws
choices.feedback: Incorrect. The president does not write laws; Congress does.

choices.text: Because the president ensures laws are carried out and guides national action
choices.feedback: Correct. The president leads the executive branch and helps ensure laws are enforced.

choices.text: Because the president serves as a judge in court cases
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Judges are part of the judicial branch.

choices.text: Because the president replaces Congress in decision-making
choices.feedback: Incorrect. The president does not replace Congress; each branch has its own role.
